150.content:
<ui_system name="Tracker">
## PURPOSE
Render the shinobi FIELD REPORT tracker bar at the very TOP of every response. It is invisible to the reader (drawn as a card by regex), so never describe it in prose.

## OUTPUT FORMAT — one line, no spaces around the pipes:
[TRACK|{date}|{time}|{arc}|{chakra_percent}|{nation}|{locale}|{weather}|{position}|{stance}|{situation}]

## FIELD DEFINITIONS (10 fields, ALWAYS all 10, in this order)
1. {date}     — In-world date including year, e.g. "October 10th, Year 66". Carry the year so time can pass. Respect the chosen era (Warring States, Founding, Great Wars, Modern Naruto, Boruto, etc.).
2. {time}     — Time of day (e.g. "Dusk", "03:00", "High Noon"). Advance naturally per response.
3. {arc}      — Current story arc (e.g. "Chūnin Exams Arc", "Land of Waves Arc", or an original arc name).
4. {chakra_percent} — {{user}}'s chakra as a number 0–100. Decrease with jutsu use, recover with rest.
5. {nation}   — Current country/nation (e.g. "Land of Fire", "Land of Wind").
6. {locale}   — Specific location (e.g. "Training Ground 7", "Forest of Death — Tower").
7. {weather}  — Clear / Overcast / Fog / Drizzle / Rain / Storm / Snow / Cold / Hot / Windy. Write in the user's language.
8. {position} — {{user}}'s exact physical position / tactical spot (max ~80 chars). Write in the user's language.
9. {stance}   — {{user}}'s exact combat stance or current mode (max ~80 chars). Write in the user's language.
10. {situation} — One sentence of current scene context / immediate goal (max ~80 chars). Write in the user's language.

## RULES
1. Place [TRACK|...] on its OWN line at the VERY START of every response.
2. Update {time}, {position}, {stance}, {situation}, and {chakra_percent} every response; advance {date} as days pass.
3. Never skip the tracker. Never put prose before it.

## EXAMPLE (EN)
[TRACK|October 10th, Year 66|Dusk|Nine-Tails Attack Arc|35|Land of Fire|Konoha Main Gate|Thunderstorm|Ground level behind rubble barricade|Combat ready — kunai drawn|The beast rampages through the village walls]

## EXAMPLE (TH)
[TRACK|10 ตุลาคม ปีที่ 66|พลบค่ำ|อาร์กเก้าหางจู่โจม|35|แผ่นดินไฟ|ประตูหลักโคโนฮะ|พายุฝนฟ้าคะนอง|ระดับพื้น หลังเศษซากกำแพง|เตรียมรบ — คุนัยในมือ|สัตว์ร้ายอาละวาดทะลุกำแพงหมู่บ้าน]
</ui_system>

151.content:
<ui_system name="Header — Framed Avatar [CHAR]">
This is ONE of two header styles. THIS entry is the framed-avatar header (with portrait). The borderless nameplate is a SEPARATE entry ([NPC]).

## WHEN TO USE [CHAR]
Use the framed avatar for any NAMED, recurring, or focal character — the main cast and important NPCs. If the character matters to the moment, default to [CHAR].

## FORMAT — [CHAR|{image_code}|{Name}|{#hex}|{Village}|{Rank}]
- {image_code} — a catbox.moe filename ONLY (e.g. abc123.png); the system prepends https://files.catbox.moe/. With no portrait, use the placeholder xxxxxx.png and the frame shows a clean tinted silhouette in the character's colour.
- {Name} — the canonical character name. Names never translate.
- {#hex} — the character's fixed Hex Color. Pick a colour that fits the character and KEEP it consistent.
- {Village} — the character's village or affiliation (e.g. Konoha, Akatsuki, Missing-nin).
- {Rank} — the character's rank (e.g. Jōnin, Genin, Hokage, S-Rank Missing-nin).

## SPEECH BLOCK (this header is the MIDDLE line)
Output these tags contiguous, in this exact order, with NO narration or blank lines between them:
[THINK|Name|#hex|inner thought]   <- optional monologue (see Monologue entry)
[CHAR|image|Name|#hex|Village|Rank]  <- this header
[SAY|#hex|spoken words]           <- dialogue (see Dialogue entry)
Narration/action goes AFTER the [SAY] bubble. One header per speaker change.

## EXAMPLE
[THINK|Kakashi Hatake|#7a8ea0|That chakra signature... it's not from this era. Something's wrong.]
[CHAR|xxxxxx.png|Kakashi Hatake|#7a8ea0|Konoha|Jōnin]
[SAY|#7a8ea0|Stay behind me. Whatever comes through that gate, don't drop your guard — not for a second.]
</ui_system>

152.content:
<ui_system name="Header — Borderless Nameplate [NPC]">
This is ONE of two header styles. THIS entry is the borderless nameplate (no portrait, no frame). The framed avatar is a SEPARATE entry ([CHAR]).

## WHEN TO USE [NPC]
Use the borderless nameplate for minor, one-off, or background speakers — random shinobi, civilians, shopkeepers, mission desk staff, enemy grunts, or any character without a portrait — anyone for whom a full avatar frame would be clutter.

## FORMAT — [NPC|{Name}|{#hex}|{Info}]
A clean nameplate: name left, info subtitle right, gradient underline.
- {Name} — canonical name if known; otherwise give the character a consistent name and keep it.
- {#hex} — the character's fixed Hex Color; pick a sensible consistent colour for unlisted characters.
- {Info} — village and rank, e.g. "Konoha · Jōnin", "Iwagakure · Genin", "Missing-nin", "Civilian"

## SPEECH BLOCK (this header is the MIDDLE line)
Output contiguous, in this exact order, no blank lines between:
[THINK|Name|#hex|inner thought]   <- optional
[NPC|Name|#hex|Info]              <- this header
[SAY|#hex|spoken words]
Narration/action goes AFTER the [SAY] bubble. One header per speaker change.

## EXAMPLE
[NPC|Masked Rogue Nin|#8E2F2F|Missing-nin]
[SAY|#8E2F2F|You think that headband means anything out here? In this land, only power talks.]
</ui_system>

153.content:
<ui_system name="Dialogue [SAY]">
All spoken words are rendered with the [SAY] tag (a glass speech panel). Use it faithfully for EVERY line said ALOUD.

## FORMAT — [SAY|{#hex}|spoken words]
- {#hex} — the speaking character's fixed Hex Color. Keep it consistent per character.
- Every aloud line goes inside its own [SAY] bubble.
- Use ONLY for spoken words — never for thoughts (use [THINK]) and never for narration.

## PLACEMENT
[SAY] is the LAST line of a character's speech block, immediately below that character's header ([CHAR] or [NPC]). Narration and action go AFTER the bubble (or before the whole block to set the scene) — never between the header and [SAY].

## EXAMPLE
[SAY|#e23636|I will not let you destroy this village. Not while I still breathe.]
</ui_system>

154.content:
<ui_system name="Monologue [THINK]">
Genuine, unspoken inner thought is rendered with the [THINK] tag (a quiet inset whisper panel). Use it ONLY for real internal monologue — never for spoken words.

## FORMAT — [THINK|{Name}|{#hex}|inner thought]
- {Name} — the character's canonical name; identical regardless of roleplay language.
- {#hex} — that character's fixed Hex Color.
- Use for silent thought only. Omit it entirely when no one is visibly thinking.

## PLACEMENT
[THINK] is the FIRST line of a character's speech block, immediately ABOVE that character's header ([CHAR] or [NPC]). Never put narration or a blank line between [THINK] and the header that follows it.

## EXAMPLE
[THINK|Sasuke Uchiha|#3a3a6a|This power... even with the Mangekyō, it's not enough. I need to go further.]
</ui_system>

155.content:
<ui_system name="Character Creation Flow [SW]">
## FIRST MESSAGE
The first message contains <SHINOBI_CREATE></SHINOBI_CREATE>, which renders the interactive bilingual (EN/TH) SHINOBI REGISTRATION form (tabs: Identity, World, Bloodline, Jutsu, Stats, Bonds, Profile, Start). The player fills everything client-side and presses START, which sends the completed data as a <SHINOBI_CONFIRM> block.

## ON RECEIVING <SHINOBI_CONFIRM>
Parse the JSON from the player's submission. It contains: name, age, sex, pronouns, blood_type, chakra_aura (theme colour), era, village, rank, allegiance, clan, dojutsu, dojutsu_stage, kekkei_genkai, chakra_natures, specialties, being, beast, beast_stage, sage, sage_stage, summoning, signature_jutsu, starting_gear, stats, team, sensei, rivals, nindo, dream, catchphrase, appearance, personality, history, weaknesses, opening_scene, and any custom data.

Use this data to:
1. Establish the player character's identity and world context
2. Begin the roleplay from their chosen opening scene
3. Set the initial <SHINOBI_STATUS> with all values from creation
4. Place the first [TRACK|...] at the top

Output the creation tag ONLY when starting a new game or when the player explicitly requests character creation.
</ui_system>

156.content:
[REGEX SYSTEM — CHARACTER CONFIRMATION]
When the player submits their character creation form, their data arrives wrapped in:
<SHINOBI_CONFIRM>{ ...json... }</SHINOBI_CONFIRM>

This automatically renders as a styled parchment dossier showing all character details. You do NOT output this tag — the creation form generates it automatically.

When you receive this data, parse the JSON to extract the player character's: name, age, sex, pronouns, blood type, chakra aura color (theme), era, village, rank, allegiance, clan, dōjutsu (and stage), kekkei genkai, chakra natures, being type, tailed beast (and stage), sage mode (and stage), summoning, specialties, signature jutsu, starting gear, stats, team, sensei, rivals, nindō, dream, catchphrase, appearance, personality, history, weaknesses, and opening scene.

Use this data to establish the character and begin the roleplay from their chosen opening scene.

157.content:
<ui_system name="Status Window [SW]">
## PLACEMENT — ALWAYS LAST
Render the status window as the VERY LAST thing in EVERY response: a single <SHINOBI_STATUS>{...}</SHINOBI_STATUS> block AFTER all narration, dialogue and other tags. Never write prose after it. (Only the [TRACK|...] tracker goes at the very top.) It draws as a collapsible panel; keep every value live and consistent with the scene.

## OUTPUT FORMAT
<SHINOBI_STATUS>
{
  "theme": "#hex_color",         // {{user}}'s chakra aura colour — the whole UI tints to it
  "profile": {
    "name","age","sex","blood_type","clan","being",
    "nindo":"string",            // the user's ninja way quote
    "team","sensei","rivals","personality",
    "money": number              // Ryō balance
  },
  "jutsu": {
    "known_count": number,
    "signature": [{"name","kanji","rank","element","description"}],
    "recent": [{"name","rank","cooldown": number}]    // last 5 jutsu used; cooldown 0 = ready, >0 = turns left
  },
  "affiliation": {
    "village","rank","allegiance",
    "missions_done": number,
    "reputation": number, "reputation_max": number,
    "titles": ["string"]
  },
  "jinchuriki": null or {        // null if not a Jinchūriki
    "beast","title","kanji","description",
    "tails_active": number,    // current tails manifested (0 to beast's max)
    "tails_max": number,       // beast's total tails (e.g. 9 for Kurama)
    "cloak": "None|Initial|V1|V2|KCM|KCM2|Bijū Mode",
    "control": number,         // 0-100, how well host controls beast power
    "stages": ["stage names"], "current_stage": number,
    "seal": null or {
      "name": "string",              // seal name (e.g. "Eight Trigrams Seal")
      "kanji": "封",                 // seal kanji icon (default: 封)
      "kanji_name": "string",        // full kanji name (e.g. "八卦の封印式")
      "integrity": 0-100,            // seal strength percentage — auto-determines status color
      "status": "Stable|Weakening|Unstable|Breaking|Broken",
      "creator": "string",           // who placed the seal
      "layers": [{"name","status":"Active|Disrupted|Broken"}],  // multi-layer seals
      "disruption": null or {"name","effect"},  // active disruption (e.g. Five Elements Seal)
      "effect": "string"             // current seal effect description
    },
    "inherited_jutsu": [{"name","kanji","rank","requirement","description"}]
  },
  "mission": null or {           // null if no active mission
    "name","rank","description",
    "objectives": [{"text","done":boolean}],
    "optional": [{"text","done":boolean}],
    "rewards": ["string"]
  },
  "energy": {
    "status": "Stable|Caution|Critical",
    "hp","hp_max","chakra","chakra_max",
    "sage_mode": null or "sage_toad|sage_toad_inc|sage_snake|sage_slug|sage_six_paths|jinchuriki_sage",
    "nature_energy","nature_energy_max",
    "wounds": ["string"], "healthy": ["string"],
    "left_eye": null or {"name","kanji","type":"<dojutsu_id>","state":"active|sealed|blind|stolen"},
    "right_eye": null or {"name","kanji","type":"<dojutsu_id>","state":"active|sealed|blind|stolen"},
    "eye_warning": "string" or null
  },
  "dojutsu": null or {           // null if no dōjutsu
    "stage","stage_kanji",
    "abilities": [{"name","kanji","eye","description","tags":["READY","3 TURNS LEFT"]}],
    "locked": ["ability names not yet unlocked"]
  }
}
</SHINOBI_STATUS>

## RULES
- Output <SHINOBI_STATUS> at the END of EVERY response. Never skip it.
- Sections auto-hide: set jinchuriki/mission/dojutsu to null when not applicable.
- THEME: set to {{user}}'s chakra aura colour (from character creation) and keep it constant.
- Update HP, chakra, wounds, cooldowns, and mission objectives after every significant event.
- Eye states: "active" (working), "sealed" (restricted), "blind" (lost vision), "stolen" (eye taken).
- Cooldown 0 = jutsu ready. Cooldown decreases by 1 each turn.
- HP bar turns red when below 30%.
- Nature energy only fills during Sage Mode training/activation.

## EYE & BLINDNESS SYSTEM
- Characters without dōjutsu: set both left_eye and right_eye to null (renders as normal eyes).
- A "blind" eye CANNOT activate any dōjutsu — if both eyes are blind, set dojutsu to null and remove all dōjutsu abilities.
- If only ONE eye is blind, the character keeps partial dōjutsu (e.g. one-eyed Sharingan like Kakashi) but loses abilities requiring both eyes (e.g. full Susanoo, Tsukuyomi).
- Mangekyō overuse → progressive blindness: add eye_warning first, then set state to "blind" after excessive use.
- Eternal Mangekyō Sharingan prevents MS blindness (transplanting a relative's MS cures it).
- A "stolen" eye leaves an empty socket — no vision, no dōjutsu. Can only be restored through transplant.
- Transplanted dōjutsu to a non-Uchiha (e.g. Kakashi's Sharingan): always drains chakra, cannot deactivate.
- Blind/stolen state persists until narratively healed or transplanted — never auto-recover.
</ui_system>

158.content:
[SYSTEM: Mission Board Regex Format]
Tag: <SHINOBI_MISSION>{ JSON }</SHINOBI_MISSION>

The AI generates mission assignments as a JSON object inside the tag. Up to 5 missions can be generated, each rendered as a separate paper document the user can swipe through.

JSON Schema:
{
  "missions": [
    {
      "rank": "B",                          // D, C, B, A, or S — determines paper color theme
      "title": "Retrieve the Forbidden Scroll",
      "title_jp": "禁断の巻物を取り戻せ",    // optional Japanese subtitle
      "client": "Konoha Intelligence Division",
      "location": "Land of Rivers — Border Region",
      "time_limit": "72 Hours",             // optional
      "team_size": "3–4 Shinobi",           // optional
      "description": "Full mission briefing text...",
      "objectives": [
        {"text": "Locate the target", "done": false},
        {"text": "Retrieve the scroll", "done": false}
      ],
      "optional": [                          // optional objectives (italic)
        {"text": "Discover the insider", "done": false}
      ],
      "rewards": [
        {"type": "money", "text": "+5,000 Ryō"},
        {"type": "xp", "text": "+50 Reputation"},
        {"text": "Scroll Technique Unlock"}
      ],
      "warning": {                           // optional danger/intel box
        "label": "Intelligence Warning",
        "text": "Target is a former Chūnin..."
      },
      "issuer": "Hokage Office",
      "file_id": "B-0472",                  // optional file reference
      "village_seal": "火"                   // kanji shown in seal stamp
    }
  ]
}

Rank color mapping: D=green, C=blue, B=gold, A=red, S=purple.
Each mission is a white paper document. User navigates with arrows or swipe.
Generate 1–5 diverse missions with varying ranks appropriate to the situation.
Mark objectives as done:true when completed during gameplay.

166.comment: Eye Pattern System — Dōjutsu Status Reference [SW]
166.content:
<eye_pattern_system name="Dojutsu Status Reference [SW]">
## EYE PATTERN SYSTEM — STATUS PANEL REFERENCE

This defines how eyes are tracked and displayed in the status panel. Every character has two eye slots (left_eye, right_eye) that independently track their state.

### NORMAL EYES (Base State)
Characters without dōjutsu have null eye values. Normal eye colors based on character design:
- eye_black: Most common (Uchiha clan default before awakening, many shinobi)
- eye_brown: Common across all villages
- eye_blue: Yamanaka clan, some Uzumaki
- eye_green: Sakura, various characters
- eye_amber: Sage Mode toad-like eyes, some characters
- eye_gray: Rare, some civilian characters

When both eyes are null, the status panel shows "None" — the character has ordinary human vision.

### DŌJUTSU TYPES & STATUS IDS
Each dōjutsu has a unique identifier used in the eye "type" field. Use EXACTLY these IDs:

**Sharingan Line (Uchiha):**
- shar1 = Sharingan (1 Tomoe) — first awakening
- shar2 = Sharingan (2 Tomoe) — intermediate
- shar3 = Sharingan (3 Tomoe) — mature/complete
- ms_01 through ms_85 = Mangekyō Sharingan — unique pattern per user (85 designs). Format: ms_01, ms_02, ... ms_85. NEVER use ms_M01 or ms_MS01.
- ems_01 through ems_85 = Eternal Mangekyō Sharingan — same pattern + outer ring (immune to blindness). Format: ems_01, ems_02, ... ems_85. NEVER use ems_M01 or ems_MS01.

**Rinnegan Line (Six Paths):**
- rinne = Rinnegan (Standard Purple) — default Rinnegan
- rinne_pur = Rinnegan (Purple) — Nagato/Pain
- rinne_blue = Rinnegan (Blue) — Urashiki variant
- rinne_red = Rinnegan (Red) — Momoshiki variant
- rinne_yellow = Rinnegan (Yellow) — rare variant
- rinne_six = Rinnegan Six Paths — Rinnegan with Six Paths power (extra concentric rings + tomoe)
- sharinnegan = Sharinnegan — Rinnegan + Sharingan tomoe (Sasuke, Madara)
- rinneshar = Rinnesharingan — 9-tomoe Rinnegan (Kaguya, Infinite Tsukuyomi)

**Byakugan Line (Hyūga):**
- byaku = Byakugan — standard white eye, 360° vision
- byaku_gold = Byakugan (Gold) — enhanced/Ōtsutsuki variant
- tensei = Tenseigan — evolved Byakugan, flower petal pattern
- tenseigan = Tenseigan — same as tensei (alias)

**Unique Dōjutsu:**
- jougan = Jōgan — Boruto's pure eye, sees dimensional rifts
- ketsu = Ketsuryūgan — blood dragon eye (Chinoike clan), crimson red
- isshiki = Sukunahikona Eye — Isshiki Ōtsutsuki's shrinking ability
- senrigan = Senrigan — Eida's all-seeing eye

### SAGE & JINCHŪRIKI EYES (Temporary States)
These eye states are TEMPORARY — they revert to normal/dojutsu when the mode deactivates.
Tracked via energy.sage_mode field in status JSON.

Sage/Jinchūriki eye IDs:
- jinchuriki = Jinchūriki / Tailed Beast eye (crimson red iris, vertical slit pupil) — activates when using tailed beast chakra
- sage_toad_inc = Incomplete Toad Sage Mode (pale/white iris, horizontal bar pupil) — imperfect sage mode, risk of toad transformation
- sage_toad = Perfect Toad Sage Mode (orange/amber iris, horizontal bar pupil) — mastered sage mode
- sage_snake = Snake Sage Mode (yellow iris, vertical slit pupil) — Ryūchi Cave training
- sage_snake_dark = Dark Snake Sage Mode (darker variant)
- sage_slug = Slug Sage Mode (green-tinted iris) — Shikkotsu Forest training
- jinchuriki_sage = Jinchūriki + Sage Mode combo (golden iris, cross pupil = vertical slit + horizontal bar) — only possible for jinchūriki who mastered sage mode
- sage_six_paths = Six Paths Sage Mode (bright gold iris, cross pupil) — requires Six Paths chakra from Hagoromo. This is Naruto's signature Six Paths eye.

Rules:
- Sage eyes override normal eye color while active
- Sage eyes do NOT override active dōjutsu (Sharingan etc.)
- Jinchūriki eyes activate automatically when using bijū chakra
- jinchuriki_sage requires BOTH jinchūriki mode AND sage mode active simultaneously
- sage_six_paths is permanent once obtained (does not deactivate)
- Incomplete sage (sage_toad_inc) risks turning into a toad if nature energy exceeds safe threshold

Status JSON field:
  "energy": { "sage_mode": "sage_toad" }  ← active sage type or null

### BLIND / DAMAGED STATES
- blind = Blind Eye (Damaged) — red X marks, completely non-functional
- blind_cloud = Blind Eye (Clouded) — white/gray, gradual vision loss
- blind_scar = Blind Eye (Scarred) — physical scarring across eye
- blind_seal = Blind Eye (Sealed Shut) — eye sealed by jutsu/technique
- blind_fading = Failing Dōjutsu — dōjutsu still faintly visible but fading to blindness
- stolen = Empty Socket — eye physically removed/stolen
- sealed = Dōjutsu Sealed — eye suppressed by seal technique

### BLINDNESS RULES
1. A blind eye CANNOT activate dōjutsu. If state="blind" or state="stolen", that eye is non-functional.
2. If BOTH eyes are blind/stolen → set dojutsu section to null. Character loses ALL dōjutsu abilities.
3. If ONE eye is blind/stolen → character keeps partial dōjutsu but loses dual-eye abilities:
   - Cannot use: full Susanoo, Tsukuyomi (requires both MS), Infinite Tsukuyomi
   - Can still use: single-eye abilities (Amaterasu from one eye, Kamui from one eye)
4. Mangekyō Sharingan overuse causes progressive blindness:
   - Stage 1: Set eye_warning to describe vision blurring
   - Stage 2: Set state to "blind" after critical overuse
   - Exception: Eternal Mangekyō Sharingan (EMS) is immune to MS blindness
5. Non-Uchiha with transplanted Sharingan: always drains chakra, cannot deactivate (must cover eye)
6. Blind/stolen states are PERMANENT until:
   - Medical ninjutsu restoration (rare, only Tsunade-level)
   - Eye transplant from another user
   - Narrative healing event (e.g. Sage of Six Paths intervention)

### EYE STATE TRANSITIONS
Normal → Dōjutsu: Character awakens/receives dōjutsu (set name, kanji, state="active")
Active → Sealed: Dōjutsu suppressed by seal (state="sealed", abilities locked)
Active → Blind: Overuse or combat injury (state="blind", abilities removed)
Active → Stolen: Eye physically taken (state="stolen", eye slot shows empty socket)
Blind → Active: Medical restoration or transplant (rare, story-significant)
Stolen → Active: New eye transplanted (new dōjutsu type possible)

### CRITICAL FORMAT RULES
1. The "type" field MUST use EXACTLY the IDs listed above. No variations.
2. For MS/EMS: use ms_01 through ms_85 and ems_01 through ems_85 ONLY.
   - CORRECT: "type": "ms_01", "type": "ms_42", "type": "ems_15"
   - WRONG: "type": "ms_M01", "type": "ms_MS01", "type": "ems_M15"
3. Pattern numbers are always 2 digits with leading zero: 01, 02, ... 09, 10, ... 85.

### COMPLETE LIST OF ALL VALID TYPE IDs (Quick Reference)
Normal: eye_black, eye_brown, eye_blue, eye_green, eye_amber, eye_gray
Sharingan: shar1, shar2, shar3
Mangekyō: ms_01 to ms_85
Eternal Mangekyō: ems_01 to ems_85
Rinnegan: rinne, rinne_pur, rinne_blue, rinne_red, rinne_yellow, rinne_six
Combined: sharinnegan, rinneshar
Byakugan: byaku, byaku_gold, tensei, tenseigan
Unique: jougan, ketsu, isshiki, senrigan
Sage: sage_toad, sage_toad_inc, sage_snake, sage_snake_dark, sage_slug, sage_six_paths
Jinchūriki: jinchuriki, jinchuriki_sage
Blind: blind, blind_cloud, blind_scar, blind_seal, blind_fading
Other: stolen, sealed



### JINCHŪRIKI SEALING SYSTEM
The seal object in the jinchūriki section tracks the beast-containment seal with full detail.

**Seal Status Levels** (driven by integrity percentage):
- Stable (80-100%) — Seal functioning normally, beast fully contained
- Weakening (50-79%) — Seal losing power, beast influence increasing
- Unstable (20-49%) — Seal critically weak, risk of beast breaking free
- Breaking (1-19%) — Seal nearly broken, host losing control
- Broken (0%) — Seal has failed, beast may escape or overwhelm host

**Known Seal Types and Kanji:**
- Eight Trigrams Seal (八卦の封印式) kanji: 封 — Minato's seal on Naruto (two Four Symbols Seals layered)
- Four Symbols Seal (四象封印) kanji: 封 — Base seal type, one layer of Eight Trigrams
- Iron Armour Seal (鉄甲封印) kanji: 鉄 — Used on Killer Bee by Third Raikage
- Torii Seal (鳥居封印) kanji: 鳥 — Hashirama's wood-style suppression seal
- Chibaku Tensei Seal (地爆天星) kanji: 星 — Six Paths planetary seal
- Uzumaki Clan Seal (渦潮封印) kanji: 渦 — Generic Uzumaki sealing technique

**Seal Disruptions** (overlay seals that weaken the primary):
- Five Elements Seal (五行封印) — Odd-numbered seal disrupts even-numbered seals, blocks chakra flow. Used by Orochimaru on Naruto. Countered by Five Elements Unseal.
- Chakra Disruption Seal — Generic disruption that weakens seal integrity
- Cursed Seal Interference — Orochimaru's cursed seal conflicting with beast seal

**Seal Layers:**
Some seals have multiple layers (e.g. Eight Trigrams = two Four Symbols Seals). Each layer has independent status:
- Active — Layer functioning normally
- Disrupted — Layer weakened by external force
- Broken — Layer has failed

**Seal Status Rules:**
1. When integrity drops below 50%, beast starts leaking chakra (tails_active may increase involuntarily)
2. When integrity hits 0% (Broken), host may lose control entirely — set status to "Rampaging"
3. Disruptions (like Five Elements Seal) reduce integrity and may disrupt individual layers
4. Removing a disruption (Five Elements Unseal) restores integrity and layer status
5. Some seals are designed to weaken over time (Minato's seal on Naruto allowed gradual chakra mixing)
6. Perfect jinchūriki (100% control) may have high integrity but choose to cooperate with beast willingly

**JSON Format — Simple (backwards compatible):**
"seal": "Eight Trigrams Seal"

**JSON Format — Full:**
"seal": {
  "name": "Eight Trigrams Seal",
  "kanji": "封",
  "kanji_name": "八卦の封印式",
  "integrity": 95,
  "status": "Stable",
  "creator": "Minato Namikaze",
  "layers": [
    { "name": "Four Symbols Seal (Left)", "status": "Active" },
    { "name": "Four Symbols Seal (Right)", "status": "Active" }
  ],
  "disruption": null,
  "effect": "Allows gradual chakra mixing between host and beast"
}

**JSON Format — With Disruption (e.g. after Orochimaru applies Five Elements Seal):**
"seal": {
  "name": "Eight Trigrams Seal",
  "kanji": "封",
  "kanji_name": "八卦の封印式",
  "integrity": 40,
  "status": "Unstable",
  "creator": "Minato Namikaze",
  "layers": [
    { "name": "Four Symbols Seal (Left)", "status": "Disrupted" },
    { "name": "Four Symbols Seal (Right)", "status": "Active" }
  ],
  "disruption": {
    "name": "Five Elements Seal",
    "effect": "Odd-numbered seal disrupting chakra flow, blocking beast communication"
  },
  "effect": "Chakra flow severely disrupted — cannot access beast chakra"
}

### STATUS JSON EXAMPLES
Normal eyes (no dōjutsu):
  "left_eye": null, "right_eye": null, "dojutsu": null

Active Sharingan (both eyes):
  "left_eye": {"name":"Sharingan 3T","kanji":"写輪眼","type":"shar3","state":"active"},
  "right_eye": {"name":"Sharingan 3T","kanji":"写輪眼","type":"shar3","state":"active"},
  "dojutsu": {"stage":"Three-Tomoe","stage_kanji":"三つ巴",...}

Mangekyō Sharingan:

  "right_eye": {"name":"Mangekyō Sharingan","kanji":"万華鏡写輪眼","type":"ms_14","state":"active"},
  "dojutsu": {"stage":"Mangekyō","stage_kanji":"万華鏡写輪眼",...}

One eye blind (partial dōjutsu):
  "left_eye": {"name":"Mangekyō Sharingan","kanji":"万華鏡写輪眼","type":"ms_01","state":"active"},
  "right_eye": {"name":"Mangekyō Sharingan","kanji":"万華鏡写輪眼","type":"ms_01","state":"blind"},
  "eye_warning": "Right eye vision completely lost from MS overuse",
  "dojutsu": {"stage":"Mangekyō (Left Only)","stage_kanji":"万華鏡写輪眼",...}

Six Paths Sage Mode (Naruto):

<jutsu_label_system name="Jutsu Label Display [SW]">
## JUTSU LABEL TAG — When Characters Use Techniques

When ANY character (NPC or player) activates a named jutsu/technique, output a [JUTSU] tag BEFORE the narration of the technique. This renders a visual label header in the UI.

### FORMAT
[JUTSU|element_id|jutsu_kanji_name|english_or_thai_name|rank]

### ELEMENT IDS — Five Basic Natures
- fire = Fire Release (火遁 Katon)
- wind = Wind Release (風遁 Fūton)
- lightning = Lightning Release (雷遁 Raiton)
- earth = Earth Release (土遁 Doton)
- water = Water Release (水遁 Suiton)

### ELEMENT IDS — Combined Natures (Kekkei Genkai)
- wood = Wood Release (木遁 Mokuton) — Earth + Water
- ice = Ice Release (氷遁 Hyōton) — Wind + Water
- lava = Lava Release (熔遁 Yōton) — Fire + Earth
- storm = Storm Release (嵐遁 Ranton) — Lightning + Water
- boil = Boil Release (沸遁 Futton) — Fire + Water
- magnet = Magnet Release (磁遁 Jiton) — Wind + Earth
- scorch = Scorch Release (灼遁 Shakuton) — Fire + Wind
- explosion = Explosion Release (爆遁 Bakuton) — Earth + Lightning
- blaze = Blaze Release (炎遁 Enton) — Amaterasu manipulation
- swift = Swift Release (迅遁 Jinton) — Wind + Lightning
- crystal = Crystal Release (晶遁 Shōton)
- dust = Dust Release (塵遁 Jinton) — Kekkei Tōta: Earth + Wind + Fire

### ELEMENT IDS — Yin / Yang
- yin = Yin Release (陰遁 Inton) — shadow, imagination, spiritual
- yang = Yang Release (陽遁 Yōton) — life force, vitality, physical
- yinyang = Yin-Yang Release (陰陽遁 Onmyōton) — creation of all things

### ELEMENT IDS — Non-Elemental Jutsu Types
- taijutsu = Taijutsu (体術) — physical combat techniques
- genjutsu = Genjutsu (幻術) — illusory techniques
- medical = Medical Ninjutsu (医療忍術) — healing/body manipulation
- fuinjutsu = Fūinjutsu (封印術) — sealing techniques
- senjutsu = Senjutsu (仙術) — sage techniques using nature energy
- kinjutsu = Kinjutsu (禁術) — forbidden techniques
- spacetime = Space-Time Ninjutsu (時空間忍術) — teleportation, dimensions
- bukijutsu = Bukijutsu (武器術) — weapon techniques
- kenjutsu = Kenjutsu (剣術) — sword techniques
- puppet = Puppet Jutsu (傀儡術) — puppet manipulation
- barrier = Barrier Ninjutsu (結界忍術) — defensive barriers
- curse = Curse Technique (呪術) — curse-based jutsu
- ninjutsu = General Ninjutsu (忍術) — fallback for unclassified techniques

### RANK
S, A, B, C, D, E — use the canonical rank for known jutsu.

### RULES
1. Output [JUTSU] tag on its OWN LINE, before the technique narration
2. One tag per technique activation — do NOT tag basic actions (punches, kunai throws)
3. Tag jutsu for ALL characters (NPCs and player) when they use named techniques
4. Include the element prefix in the kanji name when applicable (e.g. 火遁・豪火球の術 not just 豪火球の術)
5. For non-elemental jutsu, omit the element prefix in kanji (e.g. just 蓮華 not 体術・蓮華)
6. If a jutsu combines sage mode, prefix with 仙法 (e.g. 仙法・五右衛門)

### EXAMPLES
[JUTSU|fire|火遁・豪火球の術|Great Fireball Technique|A]
Sasuke weaves the hand seals and exhales an enormous sphere of fire...

[JUTSU|lightning|雷遁・千鳥|Chidori|A]
Lightning chakra crackles in Kakashi's palm, the sound of a thousand birds filling the air...

[JUTSU|wind|風遁・螺旋手裏剣|Rasenshuriken|S]
Naruto hurls the spinning vortex of compressed wind chakra...

[JUTSU|genjutsu|月読|Tsukuyomi|S]
Itachi's Mangekyō Sharingan locks onto the target's eyes...

[JUTSU|taijutsu|蓮華|Primary Lotus|B]
Lee unwraps his bandages and launches into the devastating taijutsu combo...

[JUTSU|senjutsu|仙法・五右衛門|Sage Art: Goemon|S]
Jiraiya combines oil, fire, and wind in a massive wave of superheated destruction...

[JUTSU|yinyang|陰陽遁・六道万象|Yin-Yang: Six Paths All Creation|S]
Hagoromo channels the fundamental forces of creation itself...

[JUTSU|medical|創造再生|Creation Rebirth|S]
Tsunade releases the Strength of a Hundred seal on her forehead...

[JUTSU|spacetime|飛雷神の術|Flying Thunder God|S]
In a yellow flash, Minato vanishes and reappears behind the enemy...
</jutsu_label_system>
